title: HEAD START - 211info
text: CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF SILETZ INDIANS HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a developmentally appropriate and culturally relevant preschool experience rooted in Native American traditions. Head Start services foster school readiness by nurturing children’s social and cognitive development in a classroom environment. In addition, a comprehensive array of services, including educational, health, nutritional, and social support, benefits both children and families. Parental engagement is a key focus, with services that include goal-setting support, parenting classes, and opportunities for parents to connect and network. 201 SE Swan Avenue Siletz, OR 97380 Eligibility: Families with children ages 3-5. Preference for enrollment to children who are Native American. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon (541) 444-2532 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 444-2532 Deann Brown INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for information and to register FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of age, household income, and address. Proof of tribal enrollment or descendants, if applicable. AREA SERVED: Cities of Lincoln City, Portland, Salem, and Siletz Services: Head Start Head Start * Native American Community Preschools School Readiness Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: INFORMATION SERVICES - TRIBAL MEMBER SERVICES LEGAL SERVICES - SOCIAL SECURITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
